methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Growth → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $4M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Vyriy Drone is a Growth-stage defense and security company founded by Oleksiy Babenko, having raised $4M+ through M&A activity in 2025. The broader Defense & Security sector encompasses 2 public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private peers including Anduril Industries, Helsing, Shield AI, Saronic, and Quantum-Systems, though no same-stage or adjacent-stage comparable subset met the threshold of three datapoints necessary for reliable peer median analysis.

Given data constraints, valuation relied on stage-progression methodology applied to the $4M total raised as a proxy base. This method applies a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical of Growth-stage advancement, yielding an implied valuation of $5M. The peer-median approach was deemed inapplicable due to insufficient narrow peer alignment—while 19 same-sector companies exist, none passed filters for stage proximity or valuation-tier similarity. A broad sector median would introduce unacceptable noise given the heterogeneity of Defense & Security business models and scales.

The triangulated range derived from available methods is $4M to $6M, with a median point of $5M. This analysis is constrained by reliance on a single applicable method and the absence of direct comparable transaction data at similar stage and scale. Key uncertainties center on revenue run-rate, customer concentration, and differentiation relative to the identified peer universe, particularly higher-capitalized competitors. A refreshed comp set or revenue-based validation would materially strengthen conviction in this range.
